metadata.working_title: A hippocampus-accumbens code guides goal-directed appetitive behavior

abstract_sketch.problem: Dorsal hippocampus (dHPC) encodes rich task-relevant features and projects to nucleus accumbens (NAc), yet the specific content and behavioral role of this projection-specific information stream is unknown.
abstract_sketch.gap: Prior bulk recordings in dHPC cannot isolate projection-defined subpopulations or test their causal role in appetitive behavior during goal-directed navigation.
abstract_sketch.approach: Dual-color two-photon calcium imaging of pan-neuronal GCaMP6s with retrograde mCherry labeling of NAc-projecting dHPC neurons during a spatial reward task, combined with projection-specific optogenetic stimulation and generalized linear model decoding.
abstract_sketch.key_results: NAc-projecting dHPC neurons were enriched for place cells (38% vs 32%), showed overrepresentation near the reward zone in high-success sessions, carried more speed-inhibited and appetitive-licking-excited neurons, and supported enhanced conjunctive coding (44% vs 19%) that improved reward-zone decoding; optogenetic activation of dHPC terminals in NAc elicited mouth movement and deceleration.
abstract_sketch.impact: The dHPC projection to NAc is a channel for reward-anchored spatial and behavioral-state information guiding action selection.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Hippocampus encodes environmental and task variables that support memory-guided behavior.
NAc is a downstream structure coupling motivation to action and receives glutamatergic input from dHPC.
Projection-specific coding in dHPC has been unexplored at population scale in freely behaving, goal-directed animals.
We use dual-color two-photon imaging and optogenetics to characterize dHPC->NAc neurons during a treadmill spatial reward task.

subsections.title: Mice learn a spatial reward task with anticipatory licking
content_bullets: 360 cm treadmill, 30 cm reward zone
n=18 mice, 5 training days
rewarded laps F(4)=6.344, anticipatory licking F(4)=3.803, reward licking F(4)=4.276

subsections.title: NAc-projecting dHPC neurons are enriched for place cells with higher spatial information
content_bullets: 38% vs 32% place cells chi2 p=0.012
n=6 mice, 19 sessions, 5,372 neurons (444 mCherry+), 1,750 place cells (169 mCherry+)
Welch t for spatial info, sparsity, reliability, in-PF activity

subsections.title: dHPC->NAc place fields overrepresent the reward zone during successful behavior
content_bullets: PF start p=0.033 PF end p=0.036
2-way ANOVA F_success(1,1)=54.918 p<0.001
decoding accuracy Wilcoxon p=0.0195

subsections.title: dHPC->NAc neurons are enriched for speed-inhibited cells
content_bullets: speed-excited 13% vs 16% p=0.109
speed-inhibited 15% vs 21% p=0.00022
positive regression slope 7.43e-4, r=0.937; negative regression slope -1.0437e-4, r=-0.933

subsections.title: Overrepresentation of appetitive licking-excited dHPC->NAc neurons
content_bullets: 1,268 neurons (24%) lick-modulated
lick-excited 3.8% vs 7.4% p<0.001
lick-inhibited 19.7% vs 17.1% p=0.20

subsections.title: Optogenetic activation of dHPC terminals in NAc drives mouth movement and deceleration
content_bullets: ChR2 n=4, EYFP n=3
5 mW 473 nm, 20 Hz, 5 ms, up to 10 s

subsections.title: A GLM reveals enhanced conjunctive coding in NAc-projecting neurons
content_bullets: space/velocity/lick feature importance differences
triple-conjunctive chi2 overrepresentation in dHPC->NAc
44% vs 19% conjunctive p<0.001
reward decoding Wilcoxon W(18)=14.0 p<0.001

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: dHPC->NAc neurons form a specialized information channel enriched for reward-relevant spatial and state variables.
Conjunctive coding is a candidate mechanism for downstream readout of task relevance.
Causal optogenetic evidence links this channel to approach-consummatory transitions.
Comparison to prior work on hippocampal reward overrepresentation and NAc reward coding.
Limitations: head-fixed paradigm, single projection target, no direct NAc recording.
Future directions: simultaneous pre/post-synaptic recording, behavioral manipulations, developmental and pathological states.

sections.title: Methods
content_bullets: C57Bl/6 n=16 and Thy1-GCaMP6s n=6 mice
AAVrg-pgk-Cre in NAc + AAV5-hSyn-DIO-mCherry in dHPC
AAV2-CaMKII-hChR2(H134R)-EYFP or EYFP control bilateral dHPC
LaVision TrimScope II two-photon, 920/1045 nm, 15.2 or 30.5 Hz, 1024x1024 or 512x512
NormCorre motion correction and CNMF via CaImAn v1.6.2
45 spatial bins of 8 cm; velocity>2 cm/s threshold; Savitzky-Golay filter
GLM with H2O varimp(), 3x100 shuffled null models, FDR BH
